sampling_notes.orders_date_bias: Sampling from start of sorted orders table returned 100% pass rate (all early-date rows) — not representative; guide estimate of 49% used
sampling_notes.lineitem_date_bias: Sampling from start of sorted lineitem table returned 0% pass rate (all early shipdate rows) — not representative; guide estimate of 55% used

join_order.probe: orders (filtered on o_orderdate<9204 via zone-map, ~7.35M rows)
join_order.rationale: Build on smallest side (300K filtered customers); probe orders with zone-map pruning (~51% blocks skipped)

join_order.build: qualifying orders (~735K rows, keyed on o_orderkey)
join_order.probe: lineitem (filtered on l_shipdate>9204 via zone-map, ~33M rows)
join_order.rationale: Build on 735K qualifying orders; probe 33M filtered lineitems; bloom filter pre-screens ~88% non-matching probes

filter_pushdown.index: zone_map_guided — binary search on sorted column, skip blocks with block_min >= 9204 (~51% of 150 blocks pruned)
zone_map_guided — binary search on sorted column, skip blocks with block_max <= 9204 (~45% of 600 blocks pruned)

logical_plan.output: TOP 10 by (revenue DESC, o_orderdate ASC) — min-heap of size 10, no full sort

data_structures.custkey_filter: compact_hash_set(pre_sized=524288) — open-addressing, robin-hood, holds 311K int32 custkeys from qualifying customers
data_structures.orderkey_build: compact_hash_map(pre_sized=1572864) — open-addressing, robin-hood, 735K entries, payload=(o_orderdate:int32, o_shippriority:int32), built from qualifying orders after custkey probe
data_structures.orderkey_bloom: bloom_filter(bits=8388608, ~1MB, 3-hash, ~1% FP rate for 735K keys) — L3-resident, pre-screens 33M lineitem probes, eliminates ~88% non-matching rows before hash table lookup
data_structures.aggregation: partitioned_hash_map(16_partitions) — partition by (l_orderkey & 0xF), each partition owns independent compact hash map of ~46K groups; thread-local accumulation then merge; key=(l_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_shippriority), value=double revenue_sum
data_structures.topk: min_heap(size=10) — maintains top-10 by (revenue DESC, o_orderdate ASC); built from merged aggregation result

customer_scan_and_build.note: 1.5M rows — parallel scan to collect custkeys into thread-local vectors, then sequential insert into shared compact_hash_set; small enough that build is ~2ms

orders_scan_and_probe.note: Zone-map prunes 51% of blocks; 64 threads each process ~57K rows (qualifying blocks only); probe custkey hash set; thread-local collect qualifying (o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_shippriority); merge then build orderkey hash map sequentially

lineitem_scan_probe_and_aggregate.note: Zone-map prunes 45% of blocks (330 blocks remain); 64 threads each process ~515K rows; bloom filter pre-screens before hash probe; each thread maintains thread-local partitioned aggregation map; no synchronization during scan phase

aggregation_merge.note: 16 partitions merged sequentially or by 16 threads (one per partition); final 735K groups across all partitions

index_usage.purpose: skip_blocks_where_block_min >= 9204; binary search on sorted o_orderdate to find last qualifying block; prunes ~51% of 150 blocks (~76 blocks skipped)
index_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=150][int32_t min, int32_t max per block]

index_usage.purpose: skip_blocks_where_block_max <= 9204; binary search on sorted l_shipdate to find first qualifying block; prunes ~45% of 600 blocks (~270 blocks skipped)
index_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=600][int32_t min, int32_t max per block]

customer.strategy: full_scan — only 1.5M rows × 2 columns = 7.5MB; fits in L3 (44MB)

orders.strategy: zone_map_guided_selective — binary search to first qualifying block, scan to last qualifying block; fused filter+probe+collect in single pass

lineitem.strategy: zone_map_guided_selective — binary search to first qualifying block, scan all remaining blocks; fused filter+bloom_check+probe+aggregate in single pass

memory_pattern.customer: streaming_sequential — small (7.5MB), sequential read of 2 columns
memory_pattern.orders: streaming_sequential — zone-map selects 73 blocks × 4 columns = ~116MB; sequential within qualifying blocks
memory_pattern.lineitem: streaming_sequential_with_random_probe — 330 blocks × 3 columns × 4-8B = ~990MB of sequential reads; random accesses only into 735K-entry hash map (~12MB, fits in L3)

column_load_order: o_custkey (orders join key — probe customer set)
l_extendedprice (lineitem payload — load only qualifying blocks)
l_discount (lineitem payload — load only qualifying blocks)
io_strategy.prefetch_strategy: madvise_WILLNEED on qualifying block ranges only (identified via zone map binary search before prefetch); issue concurrent madvise calls for all lineitem payload columns in parallel to overlap HDD seeks
io_strategy.hdd_notes: HDD disk — sequential access patterns are critical; zone-map pruning reduces total I/O from ~2.4GB (full lineitem) to ~1.3GB (55% of blocks); parallel madvise issues concurrent I/O requests to maximize HDD throughput

late_materialization.details: l_extendedprice and l_discount loaded in same pass as l_orderkey (no benefit to deferring since all needed per row); o_shippriority loaded with qualifying orders only (not full 15M rows)

bloom_filter.benefit: 88% of 33M lineitem probes (~29M) eliminated before expensive hash table lookup; hash table (~12MB) too large for L2 but fits in L3 — bloom filter (1MB, L3-resident) avoids most cache-miss hash probes

phase_breakdown.orders_scan_probe_collect: ~15ms (73 qualifying blocks × 4 cols = ~116MB, parallel; 7.35M rows probing 300K hash set)
phase_breakdown.lineitem_prefetch_io: ~80ms cold / ~0ms hot (HDD I/O for ~1.3GB data, parallel madvise)

estimated_cost.notes: HDD cold-start dominated by lineitem I/O (~1.3GB after zone-map pruning); parallel madvise reduces effective latency; hot run is CPU-bound on lineitem scan + bloom-filtered hash probes across 64 threads. Target competitive with DuckDB/Umbra via aggressive zone-map pruning + bloom filter + 64-thread parallelism.
